info.title: MICROSERVICE_DEV_Invoice Management API
info.description: The Invoice Management API facilitates the creation and management of invoices within a microservices architecture. This endpoint allows businesses to streamline their billing processes by enabling users to submit new invoices for various transactions. It's designed for developers integrating financial operations into their applications, ensuring seamless invoice handling for clients and internal systems. Typical use cases include automated billing systems and financial reporting tools.

post.description: This endpoint allows you to create a new billing invoice in the system. It requires relevant billing details to generate an invoice for the specified customer.

requestBody.description: Request body for creating a new billing invoice. Ensure that all required fields are populated correctly to process the invoice successfully.

400.description: The request parameters are invalid or improperly formatted.

200.description: The invoice has been successfully created and the response contains the resource details.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the error occurred.

status.description: Indicates whether the request was successful.

error.description: A numeric error code representing the type of failure.

errorCode.description: A string code representing a specific error type.

message.description: A human-readable message providing more details about the error.
message.example: The order request contains invalid or missing information.

path.description: The path of the request that resulted in the error.

traceId.description: A unique identifier for tracing the request through the system.

validationErrors.description: A list of validation errors that occurred during the request processing.

example.message: Selected speed is not available for the provided address.

suggestion.description: A suggestion for correcting the error.
suggestion.example: Please verify customer details, service availability, and billing information before submitting the order.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Cr.description: Schema representing the error response for a failed invoice creation operation.

example.message: The order request contains invalid or missing information.

validationErrors.message: Selected speed is not available for the provided address.

example.suggestion: Please verify customer details, service availability, and billing information before submitting the order.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Va.title: Validation Error

field.description: The name of the field that caused the validation error.

rejectedValue.description: The value that was rejected during validation.

message.description: A message describing the validation error.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Va.description: Schema representing a validation error encountered during the operation.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Cr.title: Invoice Creation Request

productId.description: Unique identifier for the product being ordered.

serviceDetails.description: Details of the service associated with this invoice.

address.description: Shipping or billing address for the invoice.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Cr.description: Schema for creating an invoice, including customer and product details, service options, address, and payment information.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Se.title: Service Details

serviceType.description: Type of service, e.g., internet, phone, etc.

speed.description: The speed of the internet service, if applicable.

installationRequired.description: Indicates if installation is required for the service.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Se.description: Details regarding the service being provided.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Ad.title: Address
MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Ad.description: Schema representing an address for the billing or shipping location.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Pa.title: Payment Information

method.description: Payment method used for the transaction.

billingAccountId.description: Unique identifier for the billing account.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Pa.description: Schema representing payment details for an invoice.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Cr.title: Billing Invoice POST Response

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the response indicating when the operation was processed.

status.description: Indicator of the success of the operation.

error.description: Error code indicating the type of error encountered.

errorCode.description: A specific error code associated with the operation, if applicable.

message.description: A descriptive message about the operation's outcome.
message.example: The order request contains invalid or missing information.

path.description: The endpoint path used for the operation.

traceId.description: Unique identifier for tracing the request through the system.

validationErrors.description: A list of validation errors encountered during the operation, if any.

example.message: Selected speed is not available for the provided address.

suggestion.description: A suggestion for resolving any issues related to the operation.
suggestion.example: Please verify customer details, service availability, and billing information before submitting the order.

MICROSERVICE_DEV_createBillingInvoice-Cr.description: Schema representing the response structure for the Billing Invoice creation operation (HTTP 200).

environment.name: environment
environment.description: Environment used to select the workflow branch.

serviceCategory.name: serviceCategory
serviceCategory.description: Service category used to select the workflow branch.

Vary.description: Indicates the request headers that may affect the response. This header is used for caching purposes.

Location.description: Provides the URI that refers to the newly created resource. This is typically the endpoint where the created invoice can be accessed.

content-type.description: Specifies the media type of the resource returned in the response. This allows the client to understand how to properly interpret the content.
